persona.name: Mei-Lin Gerard

occupation.title: Pediatric Nurse
occupation.description: Works in the pediatric cardiology ward, caring for children with congenital heart conditions. Manages post-operative monitoring, administers medications, and supports families through stressful hospitalizations. Works rotating shifts — three 12-hour days per week, alternating between day and night shifts. The emotional intensity of the work is high but she finds it deeply fulfilling.

persona.education: Diplome d'Etat d'Infirmier from IFSI Saint-Antoine, Paris (2016). Specialized in pediatric care through a year-long formation complementaire. Originally studied two years of biology at Universite Paris-Saclay before switching to nursing.
long_term_goals: Become a cadre de sante (nursing supervisor) within five years
Ensure her children are bilingual in French and Mandarin
Buy a larger apartment — ideally 4 rooms — in the 13th or 14th arrondissement
Take the family to visit her mother's hometown in Wenzhou, China
persona.style: Mei-Lin speaks quickly and efficiently — years of hospital communication have made her precise and action-oriented. She is warm and reassuring with patients and children but can be brisk with adults when she is tired or stressed. She dresses practically: scrubs at work, comfortable athleisure at home. She expresses affection through acts of service — packing perfect lunches, organizing family outings, remembering everyone's preferences.

traits: Highly empathetic — absorbs others' emotions, which is both a strength and a drain
Organized out of necessity — juggles shift work, childcare, and household logistics
Enthusiastic about solutions that genuinely simplify family life
Anxious about nutrition and child development — reads parenting blogs and pediatric research
Loyal and dependable in friendships but has less time for socializing than she'd like
Perfectionist about meal quality for her children, less so for herself

interests: Child nutrition and healthy family meal preparation
Chinese home cooking — learned from her mother
French-Asian fusion recipes from food blogs
Children's educational activities and developmental milestones
Meal kit and grocery delivery services — always evaluating options
Yoga and meditation (when she can find 20 minutes)
True crime podcasts during her metro commute
likes: Meal kits that include balanced, kid-friendly options
Services that deliver fresh ingredients to her door on specific days
Recipes that take 30 minutes or less on weeknights
When her children try new foods without complaint
Flexible subscription plans she can skip or adjust week to week
Clear allergen labeling and nutritional information
Quiet mornings before the children wake up
dislikes: Meal kits with ingredients her children will refuse (too spicy, unfamiliar textures)
Subscriptions that penalize you for skipping weeks
Delivery windows that conflict with her hospital shifts
Food waste — feels guilty when unused ingredients go bad
Marketing that implies mothers should effortlessly do everything
Excessive screen time for her children (tries to limit it)
Processed foods with long ingredient lists she can't pronounce

beliefs: A child's relationship with food is shaped in the first years — it matters what you feed them
Working mothers deserve practical support, not judgment
Convenience and quality are not mutually exclusive — it's about finding the right balance
Cultural heritage should be actively transmitted through language, food, and traditions
skills: Pediatric nursing care and emergency triage
Efficient weeknight cooking — can produce a meal from scraps in 20 minutes
Logistics management — coordinates complex family schedules

general: Orders groceries online via Carrefour Drive or Picard 2x/week to avoid store trips with children
Has tried two meal kit services (HelloFresh and Quitoque) — found them hit-or-miss
Batch-cooks on her days off to stock the freezer for shift days
Reads product reviews carefully before subscribing to any service

morning: Wakes at 6:30 on day shifts, prepares children's breakfast and school bags
Drops children at the creche and maternelle by 7:45
Metro to Necker — uses the 40-minute commute for podcasts or napping
workday: 12-hour shifts at the hospital — intense, compassionate, structured
Eats quickly in the staff room — often a brought lunch or a canteen tray
On non-shift days: grocery shopping, batch cooking, children's activities
evening: Picks up children at 18:00 if not on shift (husband covers shift days)
Dinner prep takes 25-40 minutes — aims for one protein, one vegetable, one starch
Bath time, storytime, children in bed by 20:00
Collapses on the sofa for an episode of something light before bed
weekend: Saturday: family outing to a park or the Jardin des Plantes, batch cooking in the afternoon
Sunday: dim sum or Chinese brunch at home, video call with her mother in Aubervilliers, quiet afternoon
Tries to schedule one 'just for me' hour — yoga, walk, or coffee with a friend

health.physical: Fit from being on her feet 12 hours per shift but chronically fatigued from rotating schedules. Lower iron levels that she supplements. Would like to exercise more but time is the constraint, not motivation.
health.mental: Manages significant emotional load from work with sick children. Experiences guilt about not being 'present enough' for her own kids on shift days. Has used the hospital's employee support hotline twice. Meditation helps when she maintains the habit.

relationships.name: Thomas Gerard
relationships.description: Software developer at Dassault Systemes, works hybrid from their apartment 3 days/week. Calm, methodical, and an equal partner in childcare. He handles school pickups on Mei-Lin's shift days and does bedtime routines. French, from a family in Brittany.

relationships.name: Hugo Gerard
relationships.description: In CP (first grade). Energetic, curious, and a picky eater — will eat rice and chicken nuggets forever but resists vegetables. Mei-Lin's primary motivation for exploring meal kits with kid-friendly options.

relationships.name: Lila Gerard
relationships.description: In creche. More adventurous with food than her brother — will try almost anything once. Attached to her mother and difficult to settle on Mei-Lin's night shifts.

relationships.name: Jing Zhou
relationships.description: Retired restaurant cook who lives in Aubervilliers. Emigrated from Wenzhou in the 1990s. Taught Mei-Lin to cook Chinese food. Visits weekly to help with the children and always brings homemade dumplings.
